row_keys._note: Maps canonical column names to actual keys in the exported JSON. The existing wmbv1_full.json uses 'wmb' and 'wmb_parent.curie'; new KG exports from CCN20230722.cypher use canonical 'node' and 'parent_curie'.

structure: Array of objects, one per node at any taxonomy level. Graph-oriented: each row represents one node with its CL mapping, parent pointer, and anatomy. All levels (class, subclass, supertype, cluster, neurotransmitter) in one file.

top_level_keys.cl: Full CL node object {identity, labels, properties: {curie, label, definition}} or null. Present on ~120 nodes: mainly neurotransmitter (all 10), subclass (~67), class (~31), sparse on supertype (10) and cluster (8).
top_level_keys.wmb: The taxonomy node: {identity, labels (→ taxonomy level), properties (node data), elementId}
top_level_keys.wmb_parent.curie: Parent node CURIE as flat string e.g. WMB:CS20230722_SUBC_285. Null for class and neurotransmitter nodes.
top_level_keys.anat: Array of {anat_id (MBA CURIE), anat_label, cell_count, cell_ratio}. Authoritative location source.

taxonomy_level_extraction.pattern: Label starting with 'CCN20230722_' — strip prefix to get level name

common_to_all_levels.notes: Flat CURIE string; null for class and neurotransmitter nodes
CL CURIE when cl node is present; null otherwise
CL term label when cl node is present
CL label string present on class/subclass even when cl object is null. Value is list — take first element.
MBA CURIE e.g. MBA:399 (renamed from loc_id in old format)
Region name (renamed from loc_name in old format)
May be wrapped in a list in source — take [0]
Fraction of type in this region; null in current file, may be populated later

cluster_and_supertype.notes: Highest mapping value — markers distinctive within parent subclass
Global cluster markers — retained as fallback
Light phase fraction; stored only when skewed (>0.7 or <0.3) — small number of types where useful for identification
Computed: round(Male/Female, 2). Null when either fraction is 0 or absent. >1 = male-biased, <1 = female-biased.
Prose CL mapping justification; list — take first element
List of DOI URLs supporting rationale
Gene list supporting CL assignment; very sparse

dropped_fields: wmb.properties.v2_size, v3_size, multiome_size, CTX_size (cell counts superseded by anat[])
wmb.properties.CCN20230722_sex_bias (categorical 'sex-dominant' flag; superseded by male_female_ratio computed from Male/Female fractions)
wmb.properties.Dark (complementary to Light; circadian_ratio captures the relevant signal)
wmb.properties.nt_type_combo_label (same as nt_type_label in most cases)
wmb.properties.neurotransmitter_accession (CCN NT accession; derivable from nt_type)
wmb.properties.neurotransmitter_marker_gene_evidence (gene list for NT call; available on request)
cl.properties.definition (long JSON-encoded prose; available in source file)

encoding_issues: File has UTF-8 BOM — strip before parsing
Literal newlines (0x0A) inside JSON string values — replace with \n before parsing
4 occurrences of double-escaped quotes (\\") inside CL definition prose — replace with \" before parsing
notes.old_format_comparison: Completely different from research/hippocampus/field_mapping.json (old format). Old format was denormalized (one row = supertype+cluster pair). New format is graph-oriented (one row per node at any level). Old field_mapping.json is obsolete for this source.
notes.cl_coverage: 120 nodes have CL mappings. cell_ontology_term (label string) has broader coverage: class 31/34, subclass 67/338, supertype 10/1201, cluster 8/5322. When cl object present, cl_id and cl_label are authoritative.
notes.neurotransmitter_nodes: The 10 neurotransmitter-level nodes (GABA, Glut, etc.) carry minimal biological information beyond their CL mappings — all cluster-specific detail (markers, anatomy, NT evidence) lives on cluster/supertype nodes. These nodes are retained solely for their CL term associations; do not use them as mapping targets. For NT type queries, prefer cluster-level find_candidates() with nt_type filter.
